title: API Contract Projection
summary: Domain DSL -> canonical API IR -> OpenAPI projection rules for shared API contract fixtures in riido-contracts.
invariants: OpenAPI is not the SSOT; if OpenAPI disagrees with API IR, regenerate or reject the OpenAPI artifact.
AI Agent v2 client API keeps generated client paths under `riido.v2.aiAgent.*` and scopes operations with `workspace_id` path parameters.
Fixture-created agents enter the normal agent lifecycle after creation; duplicate display names are allowed and are not auto-suffixed.
Figma onboarding order is client-local draft state; the API DSL must not add persisted draft state or workspace-less create.
Workspace assigned-agent profile map uses `riido.v2.aiAgent.tasks.assignedAgentProfiles` and returns current active assignment profile hints only.
delivery_rules: Generated client delivery PRs are review handoffs and must not be auto-merged by delivery automation.
Generated client path searchability is also a contract projection fact derived from `client.module` and `client.facade_path`.
Generated comments should expose both the full generated path and the module-local search path.
boundaries: This contract owns DSL schema version `riido-api-dsl.v1`, IR schema version `riido-api-ir.v1`, OpenAPI projection generation, operation ids, paths, schemas, enums, sum types, auth scope patterns, RBAC policy ids, BDD scenario ids, and generated-client lifecycle semantics.

This contract does not own control-plane HTTP handlers, request authorization implementation, RBAC evaluator implementation, persistence, stores, metrics, SSE fan-out, generated client branch mechanics, Terraform, production bearer tokens, or deployment evidence.
Device credential enrollment is a Desktop/Daemon boundary; device secrets must not be projected into generated browser clients, OpenAPI examples, events, logs, or status responses.

loop.observation: API contract fixtures already have DSL, IR, OpenAPI, and drift tests, but the projection reader was hand-maintained prose and api-contract CI did not publish JSON evidence.
loop.hypothesis: Generating the reader and evidence from checked-in API IR fixtures makes projection governance inspectable without rereading large DSL/OpenAPI files.
loop.execute: Strictly decode API IR fixtures, verify required generated paths, render the generated reader, check reader drift, and publish API contract evidence in public CI.
loop.evaluate: The verifier fails on stale generated reader, missing workflow evidence binding, missing fixture files, invalid IR, missing required generated path, or missing strict artifact upload.
loop.retrospective: API projection knowledge becomes an executable loop: DSL and IR remain the contract, generated reader explains it, and CI evidence exposes the current surface.
